unsupported_implied.text: The system shall validate the initialization file on load and, if validation fails, abort initialization, log an error, and ensure a safe known state (all units OFF).
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: "We’ll validate it on load. If it fails, we abort and log an error. ... When we abort, we still should have a safe state—everything OFF—so we don’t get stuck with units on."
unsupported_implied.reason: The source explicitly states validation-on-load and abort-on-failure with a requirement to log the error and maintain a safe OFF state. The gold set requires reading an initialization file and issuing OFF at startup but does not require validation with abort behavior or the explicit guarantee to leave the system in a safe (all OFF) state on init failure, so this is an additional, significant operational safeguard not present in the gold requirements.

unsupported_implied.text: The system shall record diagnostic/tracing information of computed boundary values (LO, UO, LT, UT) and validation status for each thermostat read in logs or traces for later diagnosis.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: “when we compute LO, UO, LT, UT, all of that should be precise and we should record those values in our logs or traces so we can diagnose weirdness in pilot if something looks off.”
unsupported_implied.reason: The stakeholders explicitly request that computed trigger/limit values and validation status be recorded in logs/traces for diagnostics. Although the gold requirements mandate computing LO/UO and LT/UT and generating events/alarms, they do not require persisting the precise computed boundary values and validation status in logs for diagnosis; this logging requirement is therefore an additional, significant capability implied by the source.
